core_mechanism: an exponentially-small spectral gap at a first-order (discontinuous) transition in the lowest eigenvalues of a parameter-tuned linear operator, arising from an avoided crossing where the ground eigenvector abruptly reorganizes onto a different state as the control parameter is swept, with the gap size obtained from a saddle-point/instanton (tunneling) estimate
brief_reason: The abstract describes a first-order quantum phase transition at which the eigenstate suddenly projects onto the unperturbed ground state and the gap between the lowest states is exponentially small in system size, computed via a two-time instanton.
